page: Microsoft Azure D192s_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor. The server is configured with 192 virtual CPUs (96 physical cores with 2.0 threads per core) and 768.0 GB of memory, yielding a balanced ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The processor includes a 480 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache, alongside advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX_TILE and AMX_INT8). This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable.

In benchmark evaluations, the D192s_v7 demonstrates top-tier multi-core performance, scoring 194674.5 in stress-ng multi-core tests and 148946.94 in PassMark CPU tests. Its memory subsystem achieves top-tier read bandwidth of 14141.15 GB/sec for small cached blocks and 673.92 GB/sec for large uncached blocks, though its overall PassMark memory score sits in the average tier. Single-core performance is also average, as shown by a stress-ng single-core score of 2239.79. However, multi-threaded operations scale exceptionally well, with bzip3 decompression reaching 879.52 GB/sec and Redis SET operations processing 263.82 million operations per second.

The server exhibits distinct performance tradeoffs, particularly in large language model (LLM) workloads. While prompt processing speeds are strong across small, medium, and large models, text generation speeds are average to weak, reflecting the lack of dedicated GPU acceleration. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the high density of 192 dedicated vCPUs and 768 GB of RAM provides substantial compute capacity per virtual machine instance, making it highly resource-dense for CPU-bound tasks. This profile is best suited for high-throughput database operations, static web serving, multi-threaded data compression, and enterprise-grade general-purpose workloads that require massive parallel processing without local storage requirements.
description: Microsoft Azure D192s_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dsv7 family, featuring 192 dedicated vCPUs (96 physical cores) and 768.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with a 480 MB L3 cache, it supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but is Premium Storage capable. Benchmarks indicate top-tier multi-core CPU performance, high memory bandwidth, and exceptional throughput for Redis and database operations, though single-core performance and LLM text generation speeds remain average. This resource-dense configuration is highly efficient for parallelized enterprise workloads, static web serving, and high-throughput database operations.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D192s_v7 is a general-purpose VM featuring 192 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 768 GB RAM, and top-tier multi-core performance, ideal for high-throughput database and enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D192s_v7 is a general-purpose VM with 192 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 768 GB RAM, delivering top-tier multi-core compute performance.
tagline: A high-density, 192-vCPU virtual machine delivering top-tier multi-core processing and massive memory bandwidth for parallelized enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a dedicated 192-vCPU Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with 768 GB RAM.
Top-tier multi-core performance and high-speed memory read bandwidth.
Supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel AMX.
Premium Storage capable configuration with no local storage or bundled GPUs.
Best suited for high-throughput database operations and parallelized enterprise workloads.
